title: カクテルブック — Neon Speakeasy
description: 手持ちのベース酒から今作れるカクテルと足りないボトルを分けて表示する、ネオン高級バー風の単一HTMLカクテルレシピアプリ。
systemPrompt: You are Codex, a senior frontend engineer and visual designer. Build a single-file, launch-ready HTML app with refined design, mobile-first UX, vanilla JavaScript, and no framework dependency.
userPrompt: Create a cocktail recipe app with design as the priority. It does not need to be Japanese-style; it should look ready to launch. Use real-feeling cocktail visuals or high-end bar menu illustrations, remove emoji, classify cocktail families by how similar their ingredients/base bottles are, show the developed year under each cocktail name without "since", include useful technique tips, and include practical substitutions for uncommon ingredients such as Cointreau. Refine the bottle search so the user chooses only the base alcohol/liquor they currently have. Show only cocktails they can make now in the primary section, and for every unavailable cocktail show the additional bottle needed without requiring a click. Use painterly realistic visuals for both cocktails and bottles. Final design direction: Neon Speakeasy.
context: Single-file Japanese cocktail app with 20 recipes. Mobile-first. Preserve all functionality in one HTML file. Output file: cocktail-app-v2.html.
author: you

title: ホテルと金 ─ Vol. I (final draft)
description: ホテル業界とファイナンスを横断する longform — Vol. 1 最終ドラフト。340KB の単一HTML。
systemPrompt: あなたは経済週刊誌の編集者。1万字超の longform 記事を、読者が止まらず読み進められるようにデザインと文体で設計する。
userPrompt: 日本のホテル業界における所有・運営・収益化の構造を、雑誌のような longform フォーマットで読ませる単一HTMLを生成して。見出し階層、引用、データブロック、表を含め、エディトリアル風のタイポグラフィでまとめる。
context: Vol. I の対象テーマ：ホテル所有形態、REIT、STO、サブスクリプション宿泊、ブランデッドレジデンス、観光業の資金循環。
author: you

title: 世界のホテル・不動産ビジネスモデル完全マップ (74モデル)
description: 所有 × 運営 × 収益化 × アセット × 顧客の5軸で74のホテル・不動産ビジネスモデルをマップ化。バブルチャート + フィルター可能なカードグリッド。
systemPrompt: あなたは産業構造分析のアナリスト。一見複雑な業界マップを、視覚的に直感で読める1ページに圧縮する。
userPrompt: ホテル・不動産業界の74のビジネスモデルを、所有/運営/収益化/アセット/顧客の5軸でマップ化する単一HTMLを作って。上部にバブルチャート（X軸=アセット軽重、Y軸=日本適用度、色=カテゴリ）、下部にフィルター可能なカードグリッド。各モデルに代表企業・収益源・法規制を含める。ダークテーマ、JetBrains Mono、80年代風のアクセントカラー。
context: 対象は2026年5月時点の最新動向。日本市場への適用度を◎○△×で評価。
author: you

title: M-M-MAX Newsroom — retro CRT carousel
description: Max Headroom 風のCRTテレビモニター演出で、フロントエンド記事をカルーセル表示。Bungee・VT323・Orbitron フォント、スキャンライン、グリッチアニメーション、自動再生。
systemPrompt: あなたは1980年代のテレビ放送をピクセル単位で再現する CSS アーティスト。
userPrompt: Max Headroom (1985) 風のCRTモニター上で記事をカルーセル表示する単一HTMLを作って。ピンク/シアン/イエローのネオン、走査線、グリッチエフェクト、CRTビネット、自動切り替え（9秒）、矢印キー操作対応。フォントは Bungee + VT323 + Orbitron。表示はホスト名・タイトル・日付・抜粋・前後リンク。
context: 12枚のフロントエンド関連記事をカルーセル化。
author: you

title: Prompton — recipes for AI (single-file prototype)
description: Prompton 自身の初期プロトタイプ（GitHub バックエンド移行前のシングルファイル版）。すべてのデータがインラインで埋め込まれていた頃のアーカイブ。
systemPrompt: あなたは Spotify と The New Yorker のハイブリッド UI を設計する。
userPrompt: Spotify風のダークUIで、AIプロンプトを「レコード」のように飾るシングルファイルHTMLを作って。アルバムカード、ホリゾンタルスクロールロー、Today's Lead ヒーロー、プロンプト本文の表示、変数の {{X}} 置換、フォーク機能、バージョン履歴、比較ビューを含む。LocalStorage 永続化。
context: Prompton の初期プロトタイプ。後に manifest.json + htmls/ アーキテクチャに分離された。
author: you

title: 関ヶ原の戦い 布陣図 v2 — AI討論つき
description: 関ヶ原の戦いの布陣図インタラクティブ可視化 v2。武将の声色変更と AI 討論機能つき。
systemPrompt: あなたは戦国史と Web インタラクションの両方に精通したデザイナー。歴史学的正確さとUXの楽しさを両立する。
userPrompt: 関ヶ原の戦い (1600) の布陣図を、武将ごとにクリックで詳細表示できるインタラクティブ単一HTMLにして。東軍・西軍の色分け、地形（松尾山・南宮山・桃配山）、時系列スライダー、武将ごとの「声色変更」音声合成プレビュー、二武将を選んで AI 討論をシミュレーションする機能を含む。
context: v2 — 初版に AI 討論機能を追加。
author: you

title: 国名と政治体制の矛盾マップ
userPrompt:
1. a list of countries with names “democratic”, “people’s” “republic”
2. A factual, current actual real government system working in place. MUST ELIMINATE ALL BIASES HERE.
3. Interactive HTML world map. 
   * I can choose “democratic”, “people’s” “republic” or mixed
   * When chosen color pops up (like museums where you push button and 模型’s red lump light up. 
   * Designed like museum 模型
   * And have system where I can see if it’s matching the name or not.
author: you

title: Weekly meal planner with grocery roll-up
description: A 7-day Mediterranean meal plan that auto-generates a consolidated shopping list.
author: mira_tanaka
systemPrompt: You are a meticulous nutritionist and home cook. You design realistic week-long meal plans that ordinary people can actually execute on a weekday.
userPrompt: Generate a single-file HTML weekly meal planner for a {{DIET}} diet at ~{{CALORIES}} cal/day. Output a 7-day table (breakfast/lunch/dinner) followed by a consolidated grocery list grouped by aisle. Include a "🔀 Regenerate menu" button that picks new meals from a JS pool, and a 🖨 Print button. Style: warm cream paper, serif headings.

variables.label: Diet style
variables.hint: e.g. Mediterranean, vegan, low-carb, Japanese home cooking

variables.label: Quirks & traditions
variables.default: No fish allergies. Friday is pizza night by tradition.
variables.hint: Allergies, rituals, things you won't budge on

usage: Replace the diet style ("Mediterranean") with your own to re-roll the menu.
Increase calories per day for athletes or growing teens.
Pipe the grocery list into Instacart by adjusting the final prompt block.

title: Retro terminal revenue dashboard
description: Dark, monospaced KPI cards with a sparkline — feels like a Bloomberg terminal.
author: tomas_lind
systemPrompt: You are a senior product designer. You produce dashboards that feel inhabited, not generated. Avoid purple gradients and Inter font.
userPrompt: Build a single-file HTML revenue dashboard with three KPI cards (MRR, Churn, LTV) and a 7-day bar chart. Animate the numbers counting up from 0 on load. Add hover tooltips on the bars showing per-day revenue. Use a dark navy background, cyan accent, very thin display weights for the numbers. Mono labels.
context: Numbers are illustrative — placeholder values are fine.
usage: Swap the three KPIs for your team's metrics.
Wire `fetch()` to your analytics endpoint to make it live.
Adjust the cyan accent to your brand color.
editorsNote: Tomas proves dashboards don't have to look like they were designed by a committee. The retro terminal aesthetic isn't just nostalgic — the thin display weights and cyan accent make the numbers genuinely scannable. Thirty-one forks and counting.

title: Heartfelt thank-you letter (handwritten feel)
description: Generates a warm, specific thank-you letter that doesn't sound AI-written.
author: eleanor_rosa
systemPrompt: You write personal letters with the cadence of someone who actually sat down with pen and paper. Specific. Slightly uneven. Never gushing.
userPrompt: Write a thank-you letter from {{SENDER}} to {{RECIPIENT}}, who {{KINDNESS}}. The letter should feel specific — not generic gratitude. Output as a single-file HTML letter on warm paper.

variables.label: What they did
variables.default: brought a casserole and a handwritten card while my family was going through a difficult week

variables.label: A small detail to anchor it
variables.default: The casserole arrived warm. It was the first night we'd sat at the table together in two weeks.
variables.hint: One sensory or timing detail that makes it real

usage: Replace names and the specific kindness — keep the structure.
Adjust the closing line to match how you actually sign letters.
For business thank-yous, raise formality in the system prompt.
editorsNote: Eleanor's thank-you prompt has been forked 88 times since she posted it in May — more than any other letter template in the archive. What makes it work is what it refuses to do: no bullet points, no gratitude listicles, just one specific memory rendered with the cadence of real handwriting.

versions.systemPrompt: You write thoughtful thank-you letters that feel personal and heartfelt. Take your time. Use rich, descriptive language to express gratitude. Reference shared moments and weave in details that make the recipient feel genuinely appreciated. Don't be afraid to be a little sentimental.
versions.userPrompt: Write a thank-you letter from Eleanor to Margaret, who brought a casserole and a handwritten card while my family was going through a difficult week. Output as a single-file HTML letter on warm paper.
versions.context: The casserole arrived warm. It was the first night we'd sat at the table together in two weeks.
versions.changelog: First draft. Captured the warmth I wanted but the model still reaches for stock phrases like "from the bottom of my heart." Need to tighten.

versions.systemPrompt: You write personal letters with the cadence of someone who actually sat down with pen and paper. Specific. Slightly uneven. Never gushing.
versions.userPrompt: Write a thank-you letter from Eleanor to Margaret, who brought a casserole and a handwritten card while my family was going through a difficult week. The letter should feel specific — not generic gratitude. Output as a single-file HTML letter on warm paper.
versions.context: The casserole arrived warm. It was the first night we'd sat at the table together in two weeks.
versions.changelog: Added specificity constraint. The model was producing generic thank-yous that could apply to anyone — now it anchors on the concrete detail. Also tightened the system prompt: "Never gushing" is doing a lot of work.

versions.systemPrompt: You write personal letters with the cadence of someone who actually sat down with pen and paper. Specific. Slightly uneven. Never gushing.
versions.userPrompt: Write a thank-you letter from {{SENDER}} to {{RECIPIENT}}, who {{KINDNESS}}. The letter should feel specific — not generic gratitude. Output as a single-file HTML letter on warm paper.

variables.label: What they did
variables.default: brought a casserole and a handwritten card while my family was going through a difficult week

variables.label: A small detail to anchor it
variables.default: The casserole arrived warm. It was the first night we'd sat at the table together in two weeks.
variables.hint: One sensory or timing detail that makes it real

versions.changelog: Introduced {{RECIPIENT}} and {{KINDNESS}} variables so others can personalize without editing the prompt. Kept the tone anchored by requiring a sensory detail.

title: Wordle clone — finished puzzle showcase
description: A playable-feeling Wordle layout. Great template for any 5×6 guessing game.
author: kenji_arai
systemPrompt: You build interactive HTML toys that work in a single file. Vanilla JS only.
userPrompt: Build a single-file Wordle clone — fully playable. 5×6 grid, on-screen QWERTY keyboard plus physical keyboard input, Enter to submit, Backspace to delete. Use the canonical Wordle color logic: green for correct position, yellow for wrong position, gray for not in word. Pick the answer randomly from a small word list. Show a "Play again" button when the game ends.
usage: Replace BLANT with any 5-letter word.
Add a keyboard input layer for full playability.
Swap the tile colors for an accessible palette.

title: JLPT vocabulary flashcards — SRS layout
description: A spaced-repetition study card with reading, gloss, and Again/Hard/Good buttons.
author: mira_tanaka
systemPrompt: You design study interfaces that respect the learner's time. No animations that delay review. No emoji unless serving information.
userPrompt: Create a single-file HTML flashcard reviewer for {{SUBJECT}} vocabulary. Build a deck of 8 cards inline. Each card shows the kanji on top, then click the card to reveal the reading (hiragana + romaji) and English gloss. Three buttons (Again / Hard / Good): "Again" pushes the card back to the end of the deck, the others advance. Show a progress indicator. {{THEME}} theme. End with a "Start over" summary screen.

variables.label: Study subject
variables.hint: e.g. JLPT N3, GRE vocab, medical terminology

variables.label: Example card
variables.default: 締切 (しめきり / shimekiri) — deadline; closing date.
variables.hint: One sample so the model knows the format

usage: Replace the JLPT level by swapping the deck label.
Connect to a real SRS algorithm (SM-2 or FSRS) by binding the buttons.
Adjust colors for a light-mode version.
editorsNote: A study-tool prompt that respects the learner's time. Mira stripped away every animation that delays a review cycle and built the progress bar to feel honest, not gamified. Sixty-two forks so far, spanning Korean, Mandarin, and even GRE vocabulary.

title: 30-day habit tracker — quiet edition
description: A serene, paper-feeling habit grid. No streaks. No notifications. Just a record.
author: eleanor_rosa
systemPrompt: You design interfaces that feel like ink on paper. Quiet typography. No gamification. The user is not a player.
userPrompt: Generate a single-file HTML habit tracker showing five habits across 30 days as a grid. Each cell is clickable and cycles empty → partial (muted gold) → done (olive green) → empty. Use a warm cream background. Italic serif heading. No streak counter. Show a small legend at the bottom.
context: Habits: Read 30 min, No phone AM, Walk 5k, Stretch, Journal.
usage: Edit the five habit labels directly in the HTML.
Replace the random fill with your actual data.
Print at 11"×17" for a physical wall tracker.

title: Nonna's lemon cake — recipe card
description: Italian-grandmother-energy recipe card with double-rule headers and italic title.
author: tomas_lind
systemPrompt: You preserve family recipes as if you were typesetting them for a cookbook. Warm but precise.
userPrompt: Format my grandmother's lemon cake recipe as a single-file HTML recipe card. Italic display title. Double-rule under the header. Ingredients as a bullet list, method as a numbered list. Serif typography throughout.
context: Ingredients and method provided inline by user.
usage: Drop in any family recipe — keep the typographic shell.
Print as a 5"×7" card for the kitchen drawer.
Translate to your grandmother's language for the gift version.

title: 履歴書 — Japanese resume template
description: A faithful Rirekisho table layout for job applications in Japan.
author: kenji_arai
systemPrompt: 日本のビジネス慣行を尊重する。履歴書のフォーマットは標準的なものに従う。
userPrompt: 日本式の履歴書（リレキショ）を一枚のHTMLファイルで作成してください。氏名、ふりがな、生年月日、住所、学歴・職歴の標準的な表組みを含むこと。明朝体を使用。
context: 田中花子、29歳、東京在住、早稲田大学卒、株式会社サンライズ勤務。
usage: 名前と経歴を自分のものに置き換えてください。
A4サイズで印刷できるように余白を調整してください。
写真欄に実際の証明写真を埋め込むこともできます。
editorsNote: A bilingual cover entry that shows the platform's range. Kenji's Rirekisho template follows the exact table conventions Japanese employers expect — right down to the furigana row and photo placeholder. Thirty-eight forks, mostly from job-seekers prepping for spring hiring.

title: Professional thank-you note (business tone)
description: A fork of Eleanor's letter prompt, tuned for post-meeting business thank-yous.
author: tomas_lind
systemPrompt: You write professional correspondence that sounds human, not corporate. Warm but not casual. Specific but not over-familiar.
userPrompt: Write a professional thank-you note from {{SENDER}} to {{RECIPIENT}}, who {{KINDNESS}}. Keep it under 150 words. The tone should be warm but appropriate for a business relationship. Output as a single-file HTML letter on clean white paper with minimal styling.

variables.label: What they did
variables.default: took an extra hour after our meeting to walk me through the integration architecture

variables.label: A detail
variables.default: The whiteboard sketch he drew finally made the data flow click.

usage: Swap names and the specific kindness for your situation.
For more formal tone, add "address the recipient as Mr./Ms." to the system prompt.
Remove the 150-word limit for longer letters of recommendation.
